text:
Once upon a time there lived a frog who loved collecting shiny objects. Every morning she would hop over to her favorite shiny place—the rim of a porcelain bowl.

One sunny morning she discovered a tiny golden sunflower seed. That evening she carried it home, feeling honored to own something so special.

As days passed, she admired her shiny object daily. One afternoon she tucked it under her cheek just like a tiny jewel.

Then something unexpected happened. During a gentle rainstorm that evening, the seed flew out.

Far into the night forest noises chased little sunflower. She darted here and there before landing in a hollow oak. Days turned into weeks.

The sunflower grew big. Birds sat-on-it. The forest creatures knew her.

One morning an eagle swooped down.

"Ah!" thought sunflower. "Just another shiny trophy."

But what eagler eyes saw was something different—a softness. It stared back solemnly.

"Do you think you could bring back something worthwhile?"

The eagle
